_cite.reason: re-point regulation primary from DG-ENV portal page to EUR-Lex enacted text (CELEX:32025R0040); a regulation's canonical source is its enacted text; prerequisite for the truncation-fix prove-on-one

prior.title: EU PPWR 2025/40
prior.summary: IN FORCE — applicable from 12 August 2026. Implementation guidance still being developed. Key delegated acts on recyclability criteria due 2028. ACTION NOW: Audit all packaging materials used for EU-bound shipments against recyclability requirements. Identify PFAS-containing materials that need replacement. Begin transition planning for reusable transport packaging. Owner: Operations + Sustainability.
prior.what_is_it: The Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (EU) 2025/40 (PPWR) replaces the 1994 Packaging Directive with directly applicable rules on packaging recyclability, reuse targets, recycled content minimums, and restrictions on certain packaging formats. Published OJ 2025/40, entered into force 11 February 2025, generally applicable from 12 August 2026. Enforced by EU Member State market surveillance authorities.
prior.why_matters: Every piece of packaging on every EU-bound shipment must comply — cartons, pallet wrap, protective materials, crating. For freight forwarders: (1) non-compliant packaging may be rejected at EU borders from August 2026, (2) wooden crating must meet both ISPM 15 AND recyclability criteria, (3) reuse targets of 40% by 2030 and 70% by 2040 for transport packaging will change procurement of crating materials, (4) recyclability criteria under Article 6 (delegated acts due January 2028) will define exactly which materials are acceptable, (5) PFAS substance restrictions affect many current protective packaging materials.
key_data: Application date: 12 August 2026 (entered into force 11 February 2025)
Recycled content: 35% for contact-sensitive packaging by 2030, 65% by 2040
Article 6 recyclability delegated acts: due January 2028
PFAS restrictions: ban on intentionally added PFAS in food-contact packaging
Mandatory EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) for all packaging placed on EU market
prior.reasoning: Affects ALL EU shipment packaging — every carton, pallet wrap, protective material from Aug 2026.
prior.full_brief:
# EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) — Regulation (EU) 2025/40

**Regulatory Fact Document** | Generated April 2026
**Primary legal text:** [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040), Publications Office of the European Union / EUR-Lex, 22 January 2025.
**Commission implementation page:** [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission, accessed April 2026.
**Commission guidance:** C(2026) 2151 Final — Guidelines on the implementation of the Regulation for economic actors and Member States, March 2026 (referenced in DG Environment implementation page; full text not in the source pool for this run — claims derived from that document are flagged where they appear).

---

# Purpose and Scope of This Document

This document covers Regulation (EU) 2025/40 on packaging and packaging waste (PPWR), which entered into force on 11 February 2025 and will generally apply from 12 August 2026. It replaces Directive 94/62/EC (PPWD), which is repealed 18 months after the PPWR enters into force; some provisions of the PPWD continue to apply after that date (specific transitional carve-outs are noted in the Confirmed Regulatory Timeline section below).

The brief is anchored to a freight-forwarding workspace whose cargo verticals include live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, and humanitarian. Transport mode priority is air primary, road secondary, ocean tertiary. Trade lanes span the Americas, Europe, and Asia. The workspace acts as freight forwarder across all verticals.

**Convention notes:**
- "The workspace" refers to the freight-forwarding operation throughout; no company or individual name is used.
- "Legal Confirmation Required" flags items requiring qualified legal counsel review before action.
- "Unconfirmed" or "Research Gap" flags items where authoritative guidance has not been published or is not in the source pool for this run.
- The full text of C(2026) 2151 Final (Commission Guidelines, March 2026) is referenced by the DG Environment implementation page but was not available in the source pool for this run. Section-by-section analysis of that document is therefore omitted. Where the guidance document is referenced, it is noted as such with a Research Gap flag.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040), Publications Office of the European Union / EUR-Lex, 22 January 2025; [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026.*

---

# What This Regulation Is and Why It Applies to the Workspace

## Plain-Language Summary

The PPWR is a directly applicable EU regulation — not a directive — governing every piece of packaging placed on the EU market, regardless of where it was manufactured or where the economic operator placing it on the market is headquartered. It covers the entire packaging life cycle: design, composition, recyclability, reuse, and end-of-life waste management.

Key substantive objectives established by the regulation:
- All packaging on the EU market must be recyclable in an economically viable way by 2030.
- Use of recycled plastics in packaging must increase safely.
- Use of virgin materials must decrease; the sector is placed on track toward climate neutrality by 2050.
- PFAS in food-contact packaging is restricted where concentrations exceed defined thresholds.
- Restrictions apply to certain single-use plastic formats (individual condiment portions, sachets).
- Take-away businesses must offer customers the option to bring their own containers at no extra cost.

The regulation is directly applicable in all 27 EU Member States from 12 August 2026. It does not require national transposition and cannot be delayed or modified by individual Member States.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040), Articles 2, 3, 10 (as referenced in existing brief preview); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026.*

## Why This Applies to the Workspace

A freight forwarder is not, by default, the entity that "places packaging on the EU market" — that role falls to manufacturers, importers, and distributors who introduce packaged goods or packaging itself into EU commerce. However, several of the workspace's operational activities create exposure that is not obvious from the regulation's headline description:

**1. Packaging commissioned on behalf of clients (importer/distributor role).**
In transactions where the workspace commissions packaging fabrication (e.g., bespoke wooden crates for fine art, custom flight cases for live events or film and TV equipment) on behalf of a client, and that packaging is introduced into EU commerce for the first time, the workspace may be the entity placing packaging on the EU market. **Legal Confirmation Required** — the precise conditions under which a freight forwarder acting as commissioning agent acquires "producer" or "importer" status under PPWR Articles 3 and 10 require qualified legal review.

**2. Reusable transport packaging managed by the workspace.**
Wooden crates, flight cases, and custom protective packaging used repeatedly across European trade lanes for fine art, live events, luxury goods, and film and TV are potentially subject to the regulation's reuse and recyclability requirements. The regulation covers all packaging regardless of material or use frequency. **Legal Confirmation Required** — whether reusable transport packaging in continuous cross-border circulation without being placed on the market in the conventional consumer sense is treated differently under the PPWR requires qualified legal review.

**3. Inner protective materials.**
Bubble wrap, foam, and other protective materials used inside crates and cases for high-value cargo across all verticals are within the regulation's scope. There is no exemption for protective packaging used in logistics or fine art contexts.

**4. Humanitarian cargo packaging.**
Packaging used for humanitarian cargo shipped into EU Member States from non-EU origins is in scope if it is placed on the EU market.

**5. High-value automotive packaging.**
Custom packaging for high-value automotive components or vehicles shipped into EU Member States is in scope.

The workspace's role as freight forwarder does not, by itself, create PPWR producer obligations. The obligation attaches to whoever places the packaging on the EU market for the first time or imports packaged goods from outside the EU. Where the workspace is acting purely as carrier/forwarder and the client retains full control of the packaging decision, the client is the regulated party. Where the workspace makes packaging decisions on the client's behalf, **Legal Confirmation Required.**

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026.*

---

# Issues Requiring Immediate Action

These items require action before or at the 12 August 2026 general application date, which is approximately four months from the date of this brief.

---

**WINDOW CLOSING — Determine which transactions place the workspace in the role of packaging producer or importer before 12 August 2026.**

The PPWR applies from 12 August 2026. Any entity that has placed packaging on the EU market without completing required producer registration will be non-compliant from day one of application. The workspace must, before that date, conduct a transaction-by-transaction review of its operations across all cargo verticals to identify those where it commissions, imports, or controls packaging decisions such that it may hold producer or importer obligations.

Action: commission a legal review mapping each transaction type (fine art crating, live events flight cases, luxury goods outer packaging, film and TV equipment cases, humanitarian cargo, high-value automotive) against the PPWR's definitions of "producer," "importer," and "fulfillment service provider." Identify which roles apply per vertical and per trade lane direction (EU-inbound vs. EU-internal vs. EU-outbound).

Deadline: complete before 12 August 2026.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

**WINDOW CLOSING — Brief clients across all EU-touching cargo verticals on their PPWR obligations and the workspace's role boundaries before 12 August 2026.**

Where the workspace's clients are the parties who place packaging on the EU market (the more common scenario for a pure freight forwarder), those clients carry the compliance obligation. The workspace's commercial risk is indirect but real: if clients are non-compliant and the workspace is providing logistics services that depend on that packaging, shipment disruption or enforcement action at EU borders or customs points is possible.

Action: prepare a client-communication package explaining PPWR scope, the client's role as producer/importer, and the workspace's role as forwarder. Confirm in writing that packaging compliance decisions rest with the client. Include contract language clarifying that the client warrants compliance with applicable packaging regulations. **Legal Confirmation Required** on contract clause language.

Deadline: complete before 12 August 2026.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

**ACTION REQUIRED — Obtain and review Commission Guidelines C(2026) 2151 Final (March 2026) for economic actors.**

The Commission published implementation guidelines for economic actors and Member States in March 2026. These are the primary authoritative guidance document for practical compliance. This brief cannot perform section-by-section analysis of C(2026) 2151 Final because it is not in the source pool for this run. The workspace's legal and compliance function must obtain this document and map its provisions against the workspace's specific transaction types.

Action: download C(2026) 2151 Final from the Commission's PPWR implementation page. Assign legal or compliance resource to map each section against the workspace's role per transaction type.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026 (references Guidelines and FAQ published March 2026).*

---

**ACTION REQUIRED — Review inner protective material specifications used in fine art, live events, luxury goods, and film and TV shipments for compliance with PPWR composition requirements.**

The PPWR's requirements on recyclability, recycled-content, and substances of concern (including PFAS restrictions) apply to all packaging including inner protective materials. Foam and bubble wrap used in high-value cargo packaging are in scope. Whether current materials specifications meet the 2026 requirements, or whether the transition period allows continued use through 2030, requires verification against the regulation's specific provisions.

Action: compile a materials inventory of protective packaging types used per cargo vertical. Map against PPWR recyclability and composition requirements. Identify any materials that may fall outside permissible specifications from 12 August 2026.

Cost impact: unknown — depends on whether current materials are already compliant or require substitution. If substitution is required, cost of alternative materials sourcing is not publicly available for this run. **Research Gap.**

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# How the Workspace Sits in the Compliance Chain

The PPWR creates a multi-party compliance chain. The workspace's position in that chain varies by transaction type.

## Role Mapping by Transaction Type

| Transaction Type | Workspace Role (Typical) | PPWR Obligation Holder (Typical) | Workspace Exposure |
|---|---|---|---|
| Fine art: client-owned crating, workspace provides logistics only | Freight forwarder / carrier | Client (producer/importer) | Indirect: client non-compliance creates shipment risk |
| Fine art: workspace commissions crating on client's behalf | Potentially: importer or producer | **Legal Confirmation Required** | Direct if workspace is deemed to place packaging on EU market |
| Live events: client-owned flight cases, workspace provides logistics only | Freight forwarder / carrier | Client | Indirect |
| Live events: workspace sources or commissions cases | Potentially: importer or producer | **Legal Confirmation Required** | Direct |
| Luxury goods: workspace handles outer packaging for EU-bound shipments | Freight forwarder | Client / brand owner | Indirect |
| Film and TV: custom equipment cases, workspace commissions | Potentially: importer or producer | **Legal Confirmation Required** | Direct |
| High-value automotive: packaging for EU-bound components | Freight forwarder | Manufacturer / importer | Indirect |
| Humanitarian: packaging for EU-inbound cargo | Freight forwarder | Sending organisation / importer | Indirect |
| Inner protective materials purchased by workspace for client shipments | Potentially: distributor or fulfillment service provider | **Legal Confirmation Required** | Direct |

**Legal Confirmation Required** — The boundary between a freight forwarder's service role and the regulated role of "producer," "importer," or "fulfillment service provider" under the PPWR is not resolved by the regulation's plain text alone. The Commission Guidelines (C(2026) 2151 Final) may clarify this, but this brief cannot confirm that without reviewing the document. Qualified legal counsel must map the workspace's specific contractual arrangements against the PPWR's definitions.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# Authoritative Guidance Document Analysis

*No content for this section as of April 2026: Commission Guidelines C(2026) 2151 Final and the March 2026 FAQ referenced by the DG Environment implementation page were not available in the source pool for this run. Section-by-section analysis cannot be performed without the document text. The workspace must obtain these directly from the Commission's PPWR implementation page.*

---

# Anticipated Authoritative Guidance and Pending Regulatory Events

The DG Environment implementation page references the following published resources as of April 2026:

- **Guidelines on the implementation of the Regulation for economic actors and Member States** — published March 2026 (C(2026) 2151 Final). Already published; the workspace should obtain immediately.
- **Frequently Asked Questions** — published March 2026. Already published; the workspace should obtain immediately.
- **Information on national competent authorities** — published on the Commission's PPWR implementation website. The workspace should identify the relevant national competent authority in each EU Member State where it operates or where clients place packaging on the market, as EPR registration and reporting obligations are administered nationally.

**Research Gap — delegated and implementing acts:** The PPWR authorises the Commission to adopt delegated and implementing acts on specific technical requirements (e.g., recyclability criteria, recycled-content calculation methodologies, labelling formats). No specific delegated acts in the source pool for this run can be confirmed as published or scheduled. The workspace should monitor the EUR-Lex legislative tracking page for PPWR secondary instruments.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026.*

---

# Threshold Questions

These questions determine whether, and in what form, PPWR obligations apply to the workspace. Each is marked as decided (resolvable from the regulation's text) or Legal Confirmation Required.

**1. Does the workspace "place packaging on the EU market" in any transaction type?**
Status: **Legal Confirmation Required.** The answer depends on the workspace's contractual arrangements. The regulation covers manufacturers, importers, distributors, and fulfillment service providers. A freight forwarder acting purely as logistics agent does not typically place goods on the market. A freight forwarder that commissions, procures, or controls packaging decisions may. Counsel must review per transaction type.

**2. Are reusable transport items (wooden crates, flight cases) that circulate continuously between EU Member States and do not enter retail commerce subject to PPWR producer obligations each time they cross a border?**
Status: **Legal Confirmation Required.** The regulation covers "all packaging placed on the EU market" but the mechanics of how repeated cross-border circulation of closed-loop reusable transport packaging is treated under the PPWR is not resolved from the source text available for this run. The Commission FAQ (March 2026) may address this. **Research Gap.**

**3. Are inner protective materials (foam, bubble wrap) purchased by the workspace and used to pack client cargo in scope?**
Status: Decided in part — the regulation covers all packaging regardless of material or use context. Inner protective packaging is not exempt. Whether the workspace's purchase and use of these materials constitutes "placing on the EU market" (triggering producer obligations) vs. downstream use of already-placed packaging (no additional obligation) is **Legal Confirmation Required.**

**4. Does PPWR apply to packaging used for goods shipped through the EU but not placed on the EU market (transit shipments)?**
Status: **Research Gap.** No authoritative guidance on transit packaging is available in the source pool for this run.

**5. Does PPWR apply to packaging used for humanitarian shipments entering the EU?**
Status: **Research Gap.** No specific humanitarian exemption is visible in the source materials available for this run. Whether exemptions exist requires review of the full regulation text and guidance.

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# Substantive Requirements

The following requirements are established by the PPWR as described in the source materials available for this run. Where the full regulation text's specific articles go beyond what the source materials confirm, claims are scoped to what is confirmed.

## Recyclability

All packaging placed on the EU market must be recyclable in an economically viable way by 2030.

Effect on the workspace's cargo verticals: Custom-fabricated packaging used across fine art, live events, luxury goods, film and TV, and high-value automotive must meet recyclability requirements. The exact recyclability criteria and testing methodology are set by delegated acts — the status of those acts is a **Research Gap** as of this run.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

## Recycled Content

The use of recycled plastics in packaging must increase. Virgin material use must decrease.

Effect on the workspace's cargo verticals: Plastic components of transport packaging (plastic foam inserts, polythene wrapping, plastic strapping) are in scope. Quantitative recycled-content thresholds and the timeline for compliance are set by delegated acts — **Research Gap** as of this run.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

## Substances of Concern — PFAS Restrictions

PFAS in food-contact packaging is restricted where concentrations exceed defined thresholds. This provision has limited direct effect on the workspace's current cargo verticals (fine art, live events, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, humanitarian) as these are not food-contact applications. However, if the workspace handles any food or beverage cargo on secondary trade lanes, this provision applies.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

## Reuse Requirements

The regulation includes packaging waste management and prevention measures and reuse requirements. A specific derogation decision exempts certain economic operators using pallet wrappings and straps from 100% reuse requirements for those packaging formats.

Effect on the workspace: Reusable transport packaging (crates, cases) used across European trade lanes may be subject to reuse targets. The specific reuse requirement applicable to transport packaging in the workspace's verticals requires review of the full regulation text. **Research Gap** as to specific quantitative reuse targets applicable to the workspace's packaging types.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en) — references derogation decisions on pallet wrappings and straps.*

## Single-Use Plastic Restrictions

Restrictions apply to certain single-use plastic formats including individual-portion condiment sachets. Direct effect on the workspace's cargo verticals is limited given that none of the listed verticals involve food service packaging as a primary activity.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

## Labelling and Identification

The regulation is supported by a decision establishing an identification system for packaging materials. Labelling requirements apply to packaging placed on the EU market.

Effect on the workspace: Where the workspace holds producer or importer obligations, labelling compliance will be required. Specific labelling format requirements depend on implementing acts. **Research Gap** as to current status of labelling implementing acts.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# Registration and Reporting Obligations

The PPWR establishes extended producer responsibility (EPR) obligations. Producers placing packaging on the EU market are required to register and report through national EPR systems administered by Member State competent authorities.

The DG Environment implementation page provides information on national competent authorities as part of its implementation support resources.

**Effect on the workspace:**
- Where the workspace is determined (following legal review) to hold producer or importer obligations for any cargo vertical or transaction type, it must register with the relevant national EPR scheme(s) in each EU Member State where it places packaging on the market.
- Registration must be in place before obligations begin applying, i.e., by 12 August 2026 at the latest — or earlier if national transposition of EPR registration procedures has already opened registration windows.
- Reporting format requirements: The DG Environment page references a Data and Reporting Directive standardising implementation reports, and a decision establishing database formats. Specific reporting templates for producer registration under PPWR EPR schemes are a **Research Gap** — the workspace should contact the national competent authority in each relevant Member State for current registration procedures.

**Research Gap — national EPR registration status:** EPR registration procedures are administered at Member State level. The specific registration portals, forms, and deadlines differ by country. This brief cannot confirm registration procedures for specific Member States from the sources available in this run. The workspace must identify the relevant national competent authority in each EU Member State where it operates.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), European Commission DG Environment, accessed April 2026.*

---

# Operational System Requirements

The following operational system changes are required or strongly implied for workspaces that are determined (following legal review) to hold producer or importer obligations under the PPWR.

## Packaging Materials Inventory and Traceability

**Scope:** All cargo verticals where the workspace commissions, procures, or controls packaging.
**Deadline:** Before 12 August 2026.
**Gap from current operational baseline:** A freight forwarder's standard operational systems track cargo, not packaging material composition. A packaging-specific materials inventory — recording material type, recycled content, supplier, and weight per shipment — is a new data layer not typically present in freight management systems.
**Action:** Build or procure a packaging materials registry that records material type, composition, recyclability status, and supplier per packaging unit commissioned or procured.

## Supplier Onboarding and Documentation

**Scope:** Packaging fabricators and materials suppliers used across fine art (crate makers), live events (flight case manufacturers), film and TV (equipment case suppliers), and any other vertical where the workspace sources packaging.
**Deadline:** Before 12 August 2026.
**Gap:** Current supplier relationships for crating and case fabrication are unlikely to include PPWR-compliant documentation (recyclability declarations, recycled-content certificates, PFAS test results). These must be requested from suppliers.
**Action:** Issue supplier information requests to all packaging fabricators and materials suppliers. Obtain recyclability declarations and recycled-content documentation. Add PPWR compliance documentation requirements to supplier contracts. **Legal Confirmation Required** on contract clause language.

## Client Contract Clauses

**Scope:** All client contracts where the workspace provides logistics services involving packaging that the client owns or controls.
**Deadline:** Before 12 August 2026.
**Action:** Add contractual provisions confirming that the client, as producer or importer of its own packaging, warrants compliance with Regulation (EU) 2025/40 and applicable EPR scheme requirements. Establish clear written record that the workspace is not acting as packaging producer on the client's behalf (unless separately agreed and properly structured). **Legal Confirmation Required** on clause language.

## EPR Registration Administration (if applicable)

**Scope:** Applies only if legal review confirms the workspace holds producer or importer status in any EU Member State.
**Deadline:** Before 12 August 2026.
**Action:** Identify national competent authority per relevant Member State. Complete EPR producer registration. Establish internal reporting process to compile packaging quantity data required for annual EPR reporting.

## Staff Training

**Scope:** Operations, commercial, and account management staff across all EU-touching cargo verticals.
**Deadline:** Before 12 August 2026.
**Action:** Brief operations teams on which shipment types potentially involve packaging subject to PPWR. Train account managers to identify client situations where PPWR obligations arise and to escalate to compliance/legal. Brief commercial leadership on client conversation parameters (see Client-Conversation section below).

*Source: [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040); [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# Exemptions and Edge Cases

## Pallet Wrappings and Straps Derogation

The DG Environment implementation page references a Commission derogation decision exempting certain economic operators that use pallet wrappings and straps from the 100% reuse requirements for those specific packaging formats. This derogation may apply to road and ocean transit operations.

Effect on the workspace: If the workspace uses pallet wrappings or straps as part of its road or ocean logistics operations, this derogation may reduce the reuse obligation for those specific items. The conditions for the derogation (what category of economic operator qualifies, what evidence is required) are not available in the source materials for this run. **Research Gap — Legal Confirmation Required.**

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en), which references the derogation decision without providing its conditions.*

## Glass Packaging Derogation

A derogation decision establishes conditions for glass packaging in relation to heavy metal concentration levels. Not directly relevant to the workspace's primary cargo verticals.

## Plastic Crates and Pallets Derogation

A derogation decision establishes conditions for plastic crates and pallets in relation to heavy metal concentration levels. May be relevant to road logistics operations.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

## Transitional Provisions — PPWD Continuation

Some provisions of the existing Packaging and Packaging Waste Directive 94/62/EC continue to apply even after the PPWD is formally repealed (18 months after the PPWR entered into force, i.e., approximately August 2026). The specific provisions that continue to apply are identified in the regulation text but are not enumerated in the source materials available for this run. **Research Gap — Legal Confirmation Required** as to which PPWD provisions continue to apply post-repeal and how they interact with PPWR requirements.

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en).*

---

# Confirmed Regulatory Timeline

| Date | Event | What the Workspace Must Have Done By This Date |
|---|---|---|
| 11 February 2025 | PPWR enters into force | (Past — no action at this milestone.) |
| March 2026 | Commission publishes C(2026) 2151 Final Guidelines and FAQ for economic actors | Obtain and review both documents; map guidance against each transaction type. |
| ~August 2026 | PPWD 94/62/EC repealed (18 months after PPWR entry into force) | Monitor which PPWD provisions continue to apply. Legal Confirmation Required. |
| **12 August 2026** | **PPWR generally applies** | All obligations in force. Legal role-mapping complete. Client communications sent. Supplier documentation obtained. EPR registration complete (if applicable). Contract clauses in place. Staff trained. |
| 2030 | All packaging on the EU market must be recyclable in an economically viable way | Packaging materials used in EU-touching operations must meet recyclability criteria. Delegated acts will specify the criteria. |
| 2050 | Packaging sector on track to climate neutrality | Long-range planning horizon only. |

*Source: [Packaging Waste — DG Environment](https://environment.ec.europa.eu/topics/waste-and-recycling/packaging-waste_en); [Regulation (EU) 2025/40](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040).*
